metadata.purpose: Literature comparison for SARTriage dissertation critical evaluation
metadata.note: Published numbers from original papers; our numbers from MASTER_RESULTS.json and tms16_results.json

Barekatain_SSD_2017.paper: Okutama-Action: An Aerial View Video Dataset for Concurrent Human Action Detection
Barekatain_SSD_2017.note: Action DETECTION (localisation+classification), not classification-only. Not directly comparable to pure classification accuracy.

YOLOv5_GBM_2022.paper: Real-Time Action Detection in Drone Videos Using YoloV5 and Gradient Boosting
YOLOv5_GBM_2022.result_description: Claimed improvement over SSD baseline but no single accuracy number published for Okutama

SCP_2024.paper: Soft Conditional Prompt Learning for Aerial Video Action Recognition
SCP_2024.result_description: +10.2% over previous SOTA without bbox, +3.17% with bbox on Okutama
SCP_2024.resolution: Full resolution with GT bounding boxes provided
SCP_2024.note: Uses prompt tuning on VLMs. Requires GT boxes — NOT applicable to operational SAR.

MViTv2-S_pixel.note: Near-chance performance (25% for 4 classes). Kappa ≈ 0 confirms no discriminative ability at <50px.

key_insight.summary: Published aerial action recognition methods achieve high accuracy (76-93% on Kinetics, 18.8% mAP on Okutama at 960×540) because they operate on large person crops (50-200px). At operational SAR drone altitudes where 96% of persons are sub-50px, pixel methods collapse to chance level (26.5%). Our trajectory-centric TMS-16+RF achieves 60.3% at these same scales — a 2.3× improvement that represents the first demonstration of viable action classification at sub-50px aerial scales.
